latestIgtvVideos.title: Phone Home Screen Organization
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
📲 You all know that I am obsessed with digital organization… So when the new iOS 14 came out I had to jump on the bandwagon and update my phone… But I DIDN’T go all out.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
I have a system that works great for me, it reduces the amount of time that I’m on my phone, it makes me more efficient, and it calms me… Why change that? 🤷‍♀️⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Updates are great. Systems that don’t work should definitely be updated. But systems that do work can be left alone.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
I’ve seen hundreds of people saying they spent hours updating their phone, and hated it and changed it back. 🤦🏼‍♀️ I’ve definitely done this my fair share of times also… But now I’m confident in the systems that I put in place.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
If a system is working for you and your family, don’t feel the pressure to change it. Feel the pressure to change the systems that are NOT working! 😌⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Did you do the update? How are you liking it 👇⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
❤️ Always, Jess.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
#digital #ios14 #ios #apple #iphone #digitalorganization #digitalinfluencer #homemaker #organization #organized #trend #trending #trendingnow #update #systems #selfconfidence #confidence #gutfeeling #change #debtfreecommunity #simplify #simplicity #simpleliving #organizedlife #organizationtips #organizationideas #cc #closedcaption #deafcommunity

latestIgtvVideos.title: Why Do I Climb Into a Freezer with 35 Degree Water?
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
🥶
 
.
Definitely NOT because it’s fun 😂 it’s a challenge- both mentally and physically- and pushes me to be better. If you challenge yourself to do hard things, you grow. Have difficult conversations, tackle hard projects, push in your workouts, and push your body in every way possible to be BETTER. It’s not easy, some days I can’t bring myself to climb in, but I’ve never climbed out wishing I didn’t.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
Ice baths push me mentally in a way I never thought possible- how calm can you keep your body in ICE COLD water? But besides the challenge, ice baths also push me to focus on my breathing, allow me to connect with my body, help my muscles recover, increases endorphins, reduce stress levels, increase alertness, increases immune response, and builds on my willpower.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
It took me years to come around to the idea, but now I try to take one almost everyday. In addition to ice baths; we also sauna, roll out, stretch, floss, scrape, cup, and use a percussive therapy device. Recovery is so important in the Haizman household!⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
What’s the hardest thing you have ever done? Hardest hike? Race? All nighter? Yoga class? 👇 Comment and brag all about your accomplishments- I want to hear!⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
❤️ Always, Jess.⠀
 ⠀
.⠀
 ⠀
#wimhof #icebath #coldbath #coldtherapy #recovery #health #fitness #workout @angigreene #girlswhoworkout #hardthings #pushyourself #meditation #breathing #hard #challenge #selflove #selfcare #diy #diyicebath #yoga #breathing #breathe #breathwork #women #womensupportingwomen #ice #iceicebaby #goals #habits #dailyhabits

latestIgtvVideos.title: How to Meal Plan
latestIgtvVideos.caption:
I share how we stick to a healthy diet and budget our groceries through taking inventory, using my printable’s, meal planning, and making a grocery list that we stick to!⠀ ⠀
.⠀ ⠀
Meal planning has changed the way we shop, and the way we eat for the better! We no longer waste food or overspend because we have all of our meals planned out.⠀ ⠀
.⠀ ⠀
Taking 10 minutes to fill these printables out each week before grocery shopping is a must in our house.⠀ ⠀

My 4-year-old still rear faces in her car seat. And she will until she outgrows it (meaning hits the weight or height limit). 

Most people don’t realize that rear facing is actually the safest position for kids for as long as their seat allows, because their neck and spine are still developing.

Forward facing too early puts a lot more force on their neck during a crash.

Every family makes their own choices, but for us this one felt like an easy decision.

Curious what everyone else does —
when did your kids turn forward facing?

#rearfacing #momlife #momsofinstagram #relatablemom #crunchymama

One of the hardest parts of motherhood for me has been realizing that my kids aren’t the only ones learning emotional regulation… I am too. 

When my toddler has a meltdown in public, it’s easy to immediately feel embarrassed, overstimulated, frustrated, or like everyone is watching.

And in those moments, my instinct is usually to “fix” the behavior as quickly as possible.

But I’m learning that most of the time, my child isn’t trying to give me a hard time…they’re HAVING a hard time.

That mindset shift alone has changed so much for me.

One thing that’s been helping lately is the Harmony at Home app because it gives practical guidance for real-life parenting situations like public meltdowns, routines, emotional regulation, and connection.

The lesson I shared in this carousel specifically reminded me to:
→ pause first
→ regulate myself
→ move to a calmer space if needed
→ focus on what my child actually needs in that moment

If motherhood has felt overwhelming lately, this app might genuinely help.

Comment APP & I’ll send you the link.

#momlife #toddlermom #relatablemotherhood #realmomlife #crunchymama @harmonyacademyed

As a mom of two, babyproofing has honestly been less about making my house look “perfect” and more about creating spaces where my kids can safely explore, climb, play, and just be kids without me feeling on edge 24/7 😅

These are the babyproofing products we actually use daily and the ones that made the biggest difference for us — especially during the curious toddler stage when they somehow find danger in EVERYTHING 🫠

I tried to focus on products that are:
• practical + easy to use
• don’t completely ruin the look of your home
• actually hold up over time
• make life easier instead of more stressful

Because the reality is… you can’t watch them every second. Having a few solid systems in place gives so much peace of mind 🤍

#babyproof #babyproofing #momhack #toddlermom #preparingforbaby

I genuinely thought we were doing pretty well when it came to reducing plastic in our home.

Then I watched a documentary and realized there were still a handful of easy swaps I’d somehow missed over the years. 😅

The goal isn’t perfection. We still use plastic. We still buy things that come in plastic. We just try to make better choices when we can.

These were a few of the easiest swaps that made me stop and say, “How did I not think of that sooner?”

Have you made any simple plastic-free swaps that you’ll never go back on?

👇 Tell me your favorite one below. I love getting ideas from this community.

Comment SWAPS & I’ll send you the link to shop these as well as my Safer Swaps guide for a full list of non-toxic swaps! 

#plasticfree #crunchymama #ecofriendly #momlife #nontoxicliving @toadandcoclothing @marleysmonsters @compostic.co @naturepedic @thrivemarket

Every family has those parenting decisions that make other people raise an eyebrow. 😅 These are some of ours. 

Will everyone agree with them? Definitely not. But every rule on this list comes from the same place: creating a home where our kids feel safe, respected, and deeply loved.

Some of these have been easy. Some have required a lot of explaining. And some have made us feel like the odd ones out. But they’re parenting hills we’re willing to die on.

Now I want to hear yours 👇

What’s one parenting rule or boundary in your home that people think is a little crazy?

If you enjoy conversations about respectful parenting, safer swaps, child development, and raising kids a little differently, make sure to follow along for more. 🤍 

#newmom #houserules #parenting #crunchymama #motherhoodunplugged

Most shoes designed for looks… not development.

Things like:
• narrow toe boxes
• stiff, thick soles
• heavy materials
• even small heels (!!)

All of that can actually affect how your toddler walks, balances, and develops over time.

Once I learned what to look for, it completely changed how I shop for shoes—and I’ll never go back to just buying the “cute” ones 🙃

If you’re shopping for summer shoes, just keep it simple:
👉 wide toe box
👉 flexible sole
👉 lightweight
👉 no heel

That alone puts you ahead of most options out there.

Comment “SHOES” and I’ll send you my favorite toddler sandal options that actually check all the boxes 👇

And follow along if you want more:
non-toxic swaps • toddler essentials • barefoot shoes • and all the things I wish I knew as a first-time mom 💛
